tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, potassium, and chloride, suggesting impaired renal function and possible mild hyperkalemia. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with abnormal urine appearance and low specific gravity, indicating possible nephropathy and metabolic derangement. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite glucosuria, which may warrant further investigation. Recommend clinical correlation for renal and metabolic status and consideration of underlying diabetes or other systemic conditions.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
